red_baseline.reason: T009 owns failure-first bounded query, cursor, correlation, redaction, and zero-write adoption inspection contracts; T010 owns their handlers.
red_baseline.summary: Final RED: the schema-bound operation.list case passes; three files fail at missing T010 modules and the existing operation handler fails the new zero/unique/ambiguous correlation contract.

validation.summary: All four T009 test files parse successfully.
1 schema test passed; 4 test-file outcomes failed only on missing T010 modules or the T010-owned correlation semantics in operation.js.
Syntax check passed for 69 JavaScript files.
No trailing whitespace in the four T009 test paths.

review.evidence: The tests use the accepted closed schemas and their narrower 15-minute/20-result/64-KiB bounds, bind cursors to trusted resource/query/high-water/expiry state, treat malformed observations as warnings only, and prove adoption inspection by before/after byte inventories. No test introduces arbitrary paths, follow mode, adoption.apply, mutation effects, or correlation-based retry.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Tests expose only the approved local query operations and enforce the accepted bounded schema limits.
Zero and ambiguous operation correlation have no next action; unique correlation exposes only get/reconcile of the original record.
Log/event tests reject arbitrary path and follow inputs and require cursor-bounded line/byte projections.
Project/task/adoption handlers receive the resolved project from trusted Kernel context; public input cannot nominate a filesystem root.
Adoption inspection uses only the trusted executable project root and ignores/cannot infer Plugin installation scope.
adoption.inspect returns a proposed plan and digest while adoption.apply remains absent from registry, results, and next actions.
Before/after project and state-home inventories plus zero effect assertions prohibit registration, journal, run, config, process, and port mutations.
Cursor tokens bind kind/resource/query/high-water/expiry; logs are byte/line bounded and redacted, malformed JSONL is warning-only.
A unique correlation result exposes only operation.get/reconcile for the original operation ID.
Zero or multiple candidates stop without retry; no next action can re-invoke the observed mutation operation.
